name: HSF-1 activity regulated lifespan and aggregation phenotypes in C. elegans
summary: Classic animal evidence supports the aging biology of heat-shock-factor signaling and proteostasis.
statement: In C. elegans, reducing hsf-1 activity shortened lifespan and accelerated tissue aging, while hsf-1 overexpression extended lifespan and small heat-shock proteins delayed protein aggregation.
population_or_model: Caenorhabditis elegans genetic aging and aggregation models
quantitative_note: The source reports directional lifespan extension with hsf-1 overexpression and requirement of HSF-1 for daf-2 insulin/IGF-1 receptor mutant lifespan extension.
caveats: This is worm genetics, not a therapeutic intervention in mammals or humans.
HSF-1 modulation can have context-specific effects and does not identify a safe human dosing strategy.
The result supports mechanistic plausibility more than human translation.
